name: Rust
description: The only aim in Rust is to survive. Everything wants you to die - the island’s wildlife, other inhabitants, the environment, and other survivors. Do whatever it takes to last another night.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS: Windows 11 64bit
  • Processor: AMD Ryzen 5 1400 or Intel Core i5-6600
  • Memory: 12 GB RAM
  • Graphics: AMD Radeon RX 470, NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060, Intel® Arc™ A580
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Network: Broadband Internet connection
  • Storage: 45 GB available space
  • Additional Notes: SSD is highly recommended or expect longer than average load times.
comment1: If you enjoy the feeling of your balls being placed in a hydraulic press, this game is a great fit for you!
comment2:
I installed Rust at 3:14 AM and immediately my refrigerator started speaking Romanian. The loading screen lasted 8 years, but emotionally it felt like a Tuesday. First thing I did was punch a tree, which caused a nearby horse to file taxes under my name.

The gameplay is unbelievable. One minute you’re building a house, the next minute a naked guy named “Soup Dentist” hits you with a rock while reciting ancient microwave frequencies. I tried farming pumpkins but accidentally joined a maritime cult and became mayor of a wolf.

Graphics? Incredible. Every sunset looks like beef jerky flavored depression. The water physics are so realistic I almost drowned in my own keyboard. At one point I heard footsteps behind me in real life and immediately crafted three wooden doors and a furnace.

The community is extremely welcoming if you define “welcoming” as being called a dishwasher by a 9-year-old with military-grade audio equipment. I made one friend but he betrayed me for half a corn and a broken flashlight. Honestly fair trade.

10/10 experience. I no longer trust forests, metal fragments, or human civilization. My doctor says I have “Rust paranoia” and legally cannot own a sleeping bag anymore.
comment3: Do not play this game if you have any hopes and dreams for your life. I am serious. This game will take everything from you. The wife... the kids... the house... even the dog... it's all gone.  Was it worth it? Maybe. I don't have the answer for that right now. I do know that I've spent over 6,900 hours of my life wasting away on this game. I haven't played a wipe in over 2 weeks and I'm still having Rust withdrawals. If you're a new player, you'll find out what that means soon enough. I've been apart of two different admin teams with 2 different orgs in the last 2 and 1/2 years and they both ended up a train wreck and ruined relationships. I've spent over 100$ on various in-game DLC over the years as well. Why did I spend 17.99$ recently on a bunch of skins for large boxes? Who knows. Maybe that's one of the reasons my wife took the kids. Anyways, I couldn't in good conscience recommend this game to any living human being. After all, this game has taken everything in my life from me. I remember nights where I would fall asleep with my headphones on full volume just so I would wake up and be able to hear if I'm getting raided... I think I might just be a psychopath but anyways... that's all I've got to say about Rust
